title: ～ぶって
titleEn: Te-form of ～ぶる, meaning 'acting/pretending to be ~'.
explanation: Te-form of ～ぶる, meaning 'acting/pretending to be ~'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: ～ぶる (in te-form: ～ぶって) indicates 'behaving as if one were ~.' This is often used negatively to criticize someone for putting on airs or pretending to know more than they do (e.g., 知ったかぶって 'to act like a know-it-all').

title: ～ぶり
titleEn: Expresses the amount of time elapsed since doing something last.
explanation: Expresses the amount of time elapsed since doing something last. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ぶり grammar point is used to express that a certain amount of time has passed since you last did something. It often translates as 'for the first time in ~' or 'after (time period)'.

title: ～ぶる
titleEn: Used when someone is acting or pretending to be something they are not (often a negative connotation).
explanation: Used when someone is acting or pretending to be something they are not (often a negative connotation). (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ぶる grammar point is used when someone pretends or acts like something they aren't. It often has a negative nuance, suggesting insincerity or putting on airs. Common usage includes nouns or descriptive words before ぶる (e.g., 学者ぶる, 偉そうぶる, etc.).

title: ～までだ
titleEn: Expresses a limit or extent; 'it's only up to...', 'no more than...'.
structure: Verb (dictionary/ない form) + までだ, い-Adjective + までだ, Noun + までだ
structureEn: Verb (dictionary/ない form) + までだ, い-Adjective + までだ, Noun + までだ
explanation: Expresses a limit or extent; 'it's only up to...', 'no more than...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～までだ grammar point is used to emphasize that something goes only up to a certain limit or extent. It can also imply 'it's only this and nothing more.'

title: ～もなんでもない
titleEn: Used to dismiss or negate something’s importance; 'it’s nothing at all', 'it doesn’t matter'.
structure: Verb-dictionary form + もなんでもない, い-Adjective + もなんでもない, な-Adjective + なもなんでもない, Noun + もなんでもない
structureEn: Verb-dictionary form + もなんでもない, い-Adjective + もなんでもない, な-Adjective + なもなんでもない, Noun + もなんでもない
explanation: Used to dismiss or negate something’s importance; 'it’s nothing at all', 'it doesn’t matter'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～もなんでもない pattern is used to deny or downplay the significance of something. It can mean 'it doesn’t matter at all' or 'it’s no big deal.'

title: ～ものとして
titleEn: Indicates an assumption or supposition; 'assuming', 'under the assumption that'.
structure: Verb-casual + ものとして, い-Adjective + ものとして, な-Adjective + なものとして, Noun + のものとして
structureEn: Verb-casual + ものとして, い-Adjective + ものとして, な-Adjective + なものとして, Noun + のものとして
explanation: Indicates an assumption or supposition; 'assuming', 'under the assumption that'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ものとして grammar pattern is used to express a supposition or assumption: 'assuming that ~', 'on the premise that ~'. It often sets up a condition or rule as the basis for some action or decision.

title: ～んがために
titleEn: Emphasizes the reason or purpose for an action; 'in order to', 'for the sake of'.
explanation: Emphasizes the reason or purpose for an action; 'in order to', 'for the sake of'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～んがために form is a formal/literary expression meaning 'in order to' or 'for the purpose of.' It attaches to the negative stem (ない-form minus ない) of the verb: e.g., する→せん, 行く→いかん, 保つ→たもたん, etc.

title: ～んばかりに
titleEn: Indicates 'as though about to ~' or 'as if to say ~'.
structure: Verb-negative stem + ん + ばかりに
structureEn: Verb-negative stem + ん + ばかりに
explanation: Indicates 'as though about to ~' or 'as if to say ~'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～んばかりに form is typically built on the negative stem of a verb (ない-form minus ない: 行か→行かん, 言わ→言わん, 知ら→知らん, etc.) + ばかり. It describes someone’s action or expression as though it were about to happen, or as though conveying a certain meaning without explicitly stating it.

title: ～差し支えない
titleEn: Indicates no problem or obstacle; 'it’s fine if...', 'there’s no issue'.
structure: Verb-て form + も差し支えない, Noun + が差し支えない
structureEn: Verb-て form + も差し支えない, Noun + が差し支えない
explanation: Indicates no problem or obstacle; 'it’s fine if...', 'there’s no issue'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～差し支えない grammar point expresses that doing something (or something occurring) is not a problem and causes no inconvenience. It’s often used in polite or formal situations.

title: ～折に
titleEn: Indicates the time or occasion when something happens; 'when', 'at the time of'.
structure: Verb-dictionary form + 折に, Verb-た形 + 折に, Noun + の折に
structureEn: Verb-dictionary form + 折に, Verb-た形 + 折に, Noun + の折に
explanation: Indicates the time or occasion when something happens; 'when', 'at the time of'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～折に grammar point is used in a somewhat formal register to express that one event or action takes place on the occasion of another. It essentially means 'when' or 'at the time of'.
